ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Oct-04-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Michael Beuselinck for and with (petitioner/administrator) Christopher Haigh.

The Court stated that it has looked at the settlement agreement and asked if counsel had anything further to add. Mr. Beuselinck responded that he has nothing further to add. The Court granted the accounting petition (subject to review of the order).

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Grimaldi. (Not Reported)

PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar Aug-16-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Michael Beuselinck for petitioner Christopher Haigh.

The Court inquired about the failure to transfer $39,000 from the attorney trust accounts into estate accounts. Mr. Beuselinck stated that the $39,000 had now been transferred into estate accounts, and that he hoped to have the accouting petition on file within 2-3 weeks. The Court stated that he wanted documentation of the deposit of the funds into the estate accounts, to be provided to the Court by August 16, 2016. The petition for approval of status report was ordered taken under submission as of August 16, 2016.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Grimaldi. (Not reported)

PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar Jul-26-2016 Continued To Aug-09-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar Jul-05-2016 Continued To Jul-26-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Michael Beuselinck for (administrator) Christopher Haigh.

The Court stated that this is an amended report of administration, that there are a couple of issues and that the additional bond needs to be filed. Mr. Beuselinck stated that they will file the bond ASAP, that there were issues on the sale of the property, that it relates to the acquisition of the bond from the bonding company, that there are issues with the personal credit and that the company has agreed to post bond if the funds are placed into a blocked account. The Court stated that the second issue is that the estates funds are in the attorney trust account, that they are not earning interest, that they need to go into an estate account and that it can be blocked if need be. Mr. Beuselinck stated that they have reached a settlement agreement with the Guardian ad Litem, that the minor has reached the age of majority and that it is contingent on a waiver of accountings. The Court stated that it is not part of the current amended report and that counsel can file another. The Probate accounting status report was continued to July 26, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 with the expectation of the funds being deposited into a blocked account with a certificate of receipt filed and bond filed. Todd Christensen of Phillips Bonding asked why a bond is necessary if the funds are being deposited into a blocked account. The Court responded that there is an outstanding order for bond.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. (Not Reported)

Petition To Determine Ownership Of Estate Property And For Order Authorizing And Directing Administrator To Transfer Estate Property.
Off Calendar May-12-2015 Continued To Jul-14-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Michael Beuselinck for Christopher Haigh. Catherine Kevane for (Claimant) Unity Technologies. Brian Delgadillo (via court call) for (Creditor) JP Morgan Chase.

The Court stated that we are here for the same issues raised in the prior civil action, that the rules suggest the civil case has priority and asked if either of the creditors appearing today have objections to the petition. Ms. Kevane stated that they want the claims adjudicated. Mr. Delgadillo stated that they were named in the civil action, that it is not going forward, that they have no objections to the underlying action, that their claim is with the decedent, that there is no dispute that the decedent had a loan, that the claim is about 1.3 million and that there are other claims on the estate. The Court stated that the claim may be more than what is in the estate, that it has a court appraisal for just under 1.3 million and that it is not sure it will proceed while the civil case is pending. Mr. Beuselinck stated that the petition has been named as a defendant. The Court stated that they may want to hire a professional fiduciary as a neutral party, that fees would be an administrative expense and that in light of the pending civil action the petitioner is wearing conflicting hats. Mr. Beuselinck stated that the purpose of the 850 petition is to streamline, that there are no objections to Christopher Haigh, that the beneficiaries are children represented by a Guardian ad Litem and that he does not represent Mr. Haigh in the civil action. Mr. Delgadillo reported that answers have been filed in the civil action. The Court stated that the civil action is contested by parties, does not see that it can sidestep the civil proceeding and suggests a continuance to see what happens in the civil action as well as to give time for Mr. Haigh to have someone step in as administrator. Mr. Beuselinck stated that the decedent had not filed taxes for 3 years and that there are issues regarding record keeping prior to her death. The Petition to Determine Ownership of Estate Property, etc., continued to July 14, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Lopes. (Not Reported)
